title: Wormia bracteata
intro: Wormia bracteata is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as broomrapes. Traditionally, Wormia bracteata is used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Wormia bracteata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Wormia bracteata is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make traditional Ayurvedic remedies.
taxonomy: Wormia bracteata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae. This family consists of about 90 species of plants, commonly known as the loganin or spikemoss family.
